fr.recommendations: Enhancing emergency responder access and operations on the property may be supported by widening or clearly marking the single access point to facilitate smooth entry and exit routes. Considering the limited rear access, ensuring that emergency pathways around the property are well-maintained and free from obstructions could aid in movement and improve operational efficiency in case of an emergency. Trimming dense vegetation, especially near the main building and along pathways, may enhance visibility and allow first responders to navigate and locate key areas more effectively. Designating clear staging areas in the front driveway or nearby open spaces would support responder positioning and equipment deployment. Additionally, ensuring clear sight lines around both the main building and detached structures might assist in swift identification of ingress and egress points, ultimately aiding in emergency navigation and coordination on-site.

wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing separation between structures and vegetation, particularly where trees and shrubs are interspersed, could improve defensible space and facilitate suppression efforts. Creating a more distinct buffer zone around buildings may reduce fuel continuity and minimize the potential for wildfire spread between structures. Maintaining an open fuel break and managing vegetation within this area can support long-term wildfire resilience and improve property preparedness. The vegetative access route should be assessed and cleared of excess growth to ensure unhindered firefighter access and enable effective response activities. Preservation and enhancement of open terrain features around the property could further reduce fire spread opportunities. Regularly reviewing and managing vegetation concentrations, especially in the vegetated perimeter, will support continued defense against wildfire exposure.
